title: QCOM QTI Bluetooth stack on Android 12
content:
It took me a while to port it to Lineage, after hours of cherry-picking, it was finally done last night.
topic:twelve-qti-bt-stack · Gerrit Code Review (lineageos.org)Funny thing about this is that CAF guys actually added proprietary features, aka bt adv audio to the open source code.
Well, I can understand that it is conditional, but some of the includes aren’t handled the same way which literally breaks the build right away.
As for bluetooth, QSSI is great: the Android 11 vendor stack works just fine, with no missing symbols or whatever.
This is probably one of the only few things that one can appreciate of.
I’ll get into WiFi display today while at it.

title: Debugging Android 12 boot
content: https://android.googlesource.com/platform/hardware/interfaces/+/b0907a6bb86e6ab67e181ac2477da8d827233d1fsrsly? this rly sucks cuz deps dont get built automatically nowKM@4.1 service was never used, however, the interface didnt get built resulting in a dlopen even for my davinci(Redmi K20) that uses KM@4.0GSI and SSI are great ideas, but those make no fun for custom roms anymore
